title: Splunk APM Service Map Health (RED Metrics: Rate, Errors, Duration) with caller-callee dependency context, span2.metrics fusion, histogram percentiles, simultaneous regression gates, inventory and topology joins, and SLO burn-rate cues
dataSources: index=metrics, observability_metrics, or otel_metrics for Splunk Observability Cloud span2.metrics style series including spans.count, spans.count.error, spans.duration.ns.p50, spans.duration.ns.p90, spans.duration.ns.p99, and related MonitorMetricSet exports; index=spans with sourcetype otel:trace, otel:span, or splunk:apm:span for parent_resource.service.name to service.name dependency edges and service.version markers; lookup apm_service_inventory.csv keyed on service identity for env, owner_team, slo_target, service_tier; index=catalog sourcetype service_topology for dependency_role enrichment; optional SignalFx smart agent or Splunk OTel Collector relays mirroring APM service view labels
app: Splunk Observability Cloud (APM), Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry Collector (Splunk_TA_otel), optional Splunk Observability Cloud export to Splunk Enterprise via HEC
description: Monitors Splunk Observability Cloud APM service map health using fused RED signals: request rate from span volume metrics, error share from error-tagged span counts, and duration tails from histogram or pre-aggregated percentile metrics, evaluated together with directional dependency context from caller to callee spans. The control highlights operations and services where rate collapses, errors rise, and tail latency inflates against environment baselines in the same window, surfaces top_caller_service and dependency_in_count for upstream blast-radius triage, and joins service inventory plus topology metadata for ownership, SLO targets, and governance routing before SLO budget burn becomes customer-visible.
value: Reliability and product leaders gain an early, map-grounded warning when a service regresses on throughput, mistakes, and tail wait time at once, which is a sharper story than latency-only outliers, error-only burn, or trace-structure gaps alone. Platform teams keep Observability Cloud and Splunk Enterprise aligned by reusing the same span2.metrics lanes investigators already trust in APM, while service owners receive rows that name the likely upstream caller, the hottest operation slice, and the next investigative drill without generic red tiles.
grandmaExplanation: Picture a busy restaurant kitchen counting tickets finished, plates sent back, and how long the slowest tables wait compared to a normal night. When finished tickets suddenly fall while send-backs jump and the slowest wait doubles together, we sound the alarm and point crews to the station and which neighbor sent the bad orders, before guests give up and leave.
implementation: Land APM span metrics in metrics-class indexes and caller-tagged spans in index=spans; maintain apm_service_inventory.csv and catalog service_topology. Save obs_uc_13_5_7_apm_service_map_red_health on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-4h@h, page when severity is critical or high after roll_rate_rps guards, and route by owner_team. Tune slo_allowed_error_pct, simultaneous RED gates, and streamstats windows per tier-zero paths.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers, service owners, and site reliability leaders who operate Splunk Observability Cloud application performance views, Splunk APM service maps, and the Splunk Enterprise indexes that receive mirrored span2.metrics style telemetry through OpenTelemetry Collector gateways, SignalFx protocol relays, or vendor-specific APM bridges. Before you tune multi-signal severity, align on what Splunk APM calls a service versus an operation: the service map aggregates health by service identity while operations remain the finest practical slice for RED interpretation, and caller_service semantics describe who initiated work toward a callee on a directed edge. OpenTelemetry resource attributes such as service.name, service.version, and deployment.environment must be stable across rollouts so metric labels and span attributes join without fragile string hacks. Parent attributes that identify upstream callers, including parent_resource.service.name when your mapping layer preserves it, are prerequisite for dependency_in_count and top_caller_service narratives that mirror interactive service map drill downs.

This use case is deliberately RED-combined and map-directional. UC-13.5.1 remains the authoritative home for duration-first percentile dwell, tail-sampling drift language, and slow-path hotspot framing without requiring simultaneous rate and error regression. UC-13.5.2 remains error-rate-first with burn-rate emphasis across span lanes, span events, metrics, and Web 5xx complements, without mandating concurrent duration ratio gates. UC-13.5.3 remains structural completeness for orphan spans, missing roots, collector drops, and propagation fractures that make graphs untrustworthy. If UC-13.5.3 alarms are chronic, treat service map RED conclusions as hypothesis-only until trace linkage stabilizes, because a lying graph can fabricate plausible rate and duration movement from missing parents rather than true customer pain.

Understand Splunk APM metric layers before you wire alerts. Raw span-derived metrics often arrive under families associated with span2.metrics naming in Observability Cloud materializations, while MonitorMetricSets and related MetricSet rollups publish percentile surfaces that trade cardinality for query speed. Histogram-native OpenTelemetry exports expose bucket counts that Splunk APM or downstream pipelines may translate into p50, p90, and p99 estimates; aggregated metrics may instead ship precomputed quantiles that drift when histogram boundaries change. Document which lane is authoritative per environment so investigators do not argue about two percentile feeds that measure slightly different populations.

Detector and Signalflow posture matters for operational closure. Observability Cloud detectors commonly evaluate MetricSets on rolling windows, and Signalflow programs express transformations that can be mirrored as governance references when Splunk Enterprise saved searches remain the system of record for evidence packs. This UC bridges narrative: Splunk SPL correlates Enterprise-stored metrics and spans, while teams may still clone thresholds into Observability Cloud detectors for faster graph-native paging. Keep threshold parity documented so on-call engineers do not receive divergent severities for the same underlying regression.

SLO objective definitions belong in prerequisites, not as an afterthought. Express allowed error share as a small percent for tier-one operations, map it to slo_allowed_error_pct in SPL, and translate objectives into slo_burn_rate comparisons that escalate when short-window error share consumes the budget too quickly. Pair objectives with inventory columns slo_target and service_tier from apm_service_inventory.csv so tier-zero paths receive stricter simultaneous RED gates than back-office reporting. Legal and privacy review must cover high-cardinality labels that can carry customer segments; hash or strip at collectors when counsel requires minimization, and restrict trace and metric indexes to engineering identities with job need.

Risk briefing for leadership: simultaneous rate collapse, error share growth, and tail duration inflation often indicate a canary or dependency regression, but they can also reflect sampling edits, mis-tagged service.version splits, or an upstream traffic shift that reduces calls while errors concentrate on a thin slice. Prepare executives for the idea that dependency_in_count growth without errors can be healthy platform evolution, while a hot edge with errors and latency growth demands map-first triage before blaming a single microservice in isolation.

Differentiation recap: simultaneous RED regression logic with directional dependency context, histogram versus pre-aggregated percentile awareness, inventory and topology joins, service map drill-down URL habits described in runbooks, and explicit bridge language to Observability Cloud detectors and Signalflow, not pure duration-only alarms, not pure error-only burn, and not orphan-span completeness analytics.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands span volume metrics that back RED Rate in metrics-class indexes such as metrics, observability_metrics, or otel_metrics. Prefer native Observability Cloud exports that preserve service and operation dimensions compatible with Splunk APM service map tiles. Normalize metric_name values that correspond to successful span counts, including spans.count style names and span2.metrics aligned variants your tenant actually emits. Preserve sf_service, sf_operation, and sf_environment style facets when present because they often match APM UI filters verbatim.

Lane B lands error-bearing counts that back RED Errors, including spans.count.error when published distinctly, or spans.count rows with sf_error true when your pipeline encodes failures that way. Align error semantics with UC-13.5.2 so investigators see consistent is_err logic across span searches and metric searches. Keep exception cardinality out of metric labels to avoid cost explosions.

Lane C lands duration signals that back RED Duration, including spans.duration.ns.p50, spans.duration.ns.p90, spans.duration.ns.p99, spans.duration.median, or tenant-specific histogram rollups. When only nanosecond counters exist, convert at search time with disciplined division to milliseconds for human-readable thresholds. When OpenTelemetry histogram export is authoritative, document bucket boundaries and exporter version because percentile shifts can be histogram re-binning rather than code regressions.

Lane D lands caller-callee edges from index=spans with sourcetypes such as otel:trace, otel:span, or splunk:apm:span. Map parent_resource.service.name or caller_service to caller and service.name to callee so dependency_in_count reflects distinct upstreams touching a service during a window. This lane is how Splunk rows acquire top_caller_service hints that match service map upstream badges when fields are complete.

Lane E lands lightweight version markers from spans, including service.version, build_id, or image_tag when your Kubernetes manifests populate them, so joins surface version skew during canary windows even when metric lanes omit version labels temporarily.

HTTP Event Collector tokens and OTLP receivers require rate limits, retry visibility, and queue health monitoring. Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector pipelines should use attribute processors to normalize http.route and strip secrets. Document index macros for each tenant so the saved search is portable.

Example readiness checks: verify a canary service shows coherent spans.count and error metrics with identical service_name keys, verify histogram percentiles move in the APM UI when you inject latency in lab, verify apm_service_inventory.csv loads through inputlookup without type mismatches, and verify catalog service_topology contains deduped rows for tier-one services.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as obs_uc_13_5_7_apm_service_map_red_health with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after volume guards. Throttle duplicate service_name, operation, and environment tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Include recommended_action, owner_team, top_caller_service, and slo_burn_rate in pager text so responders open the correct APM service map drill down without ad hoc guesswork. Encode a deep-link pattern in your runbook that maps service_name and environment to the Splunk Observability Cloud APM service view URL template your organization standardizes, including URL-encoded query parameters for operation facets when available.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms for span2.metrics-compatible rate counts, error counts, duration percentile metrics, dependency edge enumeration from spans, and version marker spans. coalesce ladders absorb field naming drift across OTLP flattenings, SignalFx-style facets, and APM bridge exports. bin enforces five-minute buckets aligned to service map rollups. stats fuses arms per service_name, operation, environment, and service_version. streamstats implements short-window momentum for rate_rps and error_rate so simultaneous regressions require sustained behavior rather than single bucket noise. eventstats establishes baseline_p99_ms per service_name and environment for ratio tests. case encodes critical simultaneous regression when rate_drop_pct exceeds forty percent with error_rate above one percent and p99_ratio at least two, plus rolling rate guards. inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv enriches owner_team, slo_target, env, and service_tier. join against catalog service_topology adds dependency_role for governance context alongside RED.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.7 Splunk APM service map RED fusion: span2.metrics rate and error lanes, duration percentiles, caller-callee dependency edges, optional version lane. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h bin=5m slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1 critical_combo_rate_drop_pct=40 critical_combo_err_pct=1.0 critical_combo_p99_mult=2.0")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name="spans.count" OR match(lower(metric_name),"^spans\.count$") OR match(lower(metric_name),"span2\.metrics.*spans\.count"))
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_operation,span_name,http_route,sf_operation,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,sf_environment,env,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,sf_service_version,release,"unknown_version")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval span_total=if(mv<0,-1*mv,mv)
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_span2_rate"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name="spans.count.error" OR (metric_name="spans.count" AND sf_error="true") OR match(lower(metric_name),"spans\.count\.error"))
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_operation,span_name,sf_operation,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,sf_environment,env,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,sf_service_version,release,"unknown_version")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval err_total=if(mv<0,-1*mv,mv)
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_span2_error"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name="spans.duration.ns.p50" OR metric_name="spans.duration.ns.p90" OR metric_name="spans.duration.ns.p99" OR metric_name="spans.duration.median" OR match(lower(metric_name),"spans\.duration"))
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_operation,span_name,sf_operation,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,sf_environment,env,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,sf_service_version,release,"unknown_version")
      | eval nsv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval p50_ms=if(match(lower(metric_name),"p50|median|p\\.50"),round(nsv/1000000,4),0)
      | eval p90_ms=if(match(lower(metric_name),"p90|p\\.90"),round(nsv/1000000,4),0)
      | eval p99_ms=if(match(lower(metric_name),"p99|p\\.99"),round(nsv/1000000,4),0)
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_span2_duration_pct"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span OR sourcetype=splunk:apm:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,service,""))))
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('parent_resource.service.name','caller.service.name',caller_service,upstream_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(name,span_name,operation_name,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unknown_version")
      | where len(callee)>0 AND len(caller)>0 AND caller!=callee
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats dc(caller) AS dependency_in_count values(caller) AS caller_mv BY _time callee operation environment service_version
      | eval top_caller_service=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(caller_mv),0),"none_observed")
      | rename callee AS service_name
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval lane="apm_dependency_edge"
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(name,span_name,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,build_id,image_tag,"unknown_version")
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_version_marker"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
| eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,"unknown_version")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(span_total) AS span_total sum(err_total) AS err_total
    max(p50_ms) AS p50_ms max(p90_ms) AS p90_ms max(p99_ms) AS p99_ms
    max(dependency_in_count) AS dependency_in_count
    values(top_caller_service) AS caller_mv
    BY _time service_name operation environment service_version
| eval top_caller_service=coalesce(mvindex(mvfilter(match(caller_mv,".+")),0),mvindex(caller_mv,0),"none_observed")
| eval rate_rps=round(span_total/300,6)
| eval span_total=if(isnull(span_total) OR span_total<1,1,span_total)
| eval error_rate=round(100*err_total/span_total,4)
| sort 0 + service_name + operation + environment + _time
| streamstats window=12 current=f global=f last(rate_rps) AS prior_rate_rps last(p99_ms) AS prior_p99_ms BY service_name operation environment service_version
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f avg(rate_rps) AS roll_rate_rps avg(error_rate) AS roll_err_rate BY service_name operation environment service_version
| eventstats avg(p99_ms) AS baseline_p99_ms BY service_name environment
| eval baseline_p99_ms=coalesce(baseline_p99_ms,prior_p99_ms,p99_ms,0.0001)
| eval rate_drop_pct=if(prior_rate_rps>0, round(100*(prior_rate_rps-rate_rps)/prior_rate_rps,2), 0)
| eval p99_ratio=if(baseline_p99_ms>0, round(p99_ms/baseline_p99_ms,3), 1)
| eval slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1
| eval slo_burn_rate=round(error_rate/nullif(slo_allowed_error_pct,0),4)
| eval severity=case(
    rate_drop_pct>40 AND error_rate>1.0 AND p99_ratio>=2.0 AND roll_rate_rps>0.01, "critical",
    rate_drop_pct>25 AND error_rate>0.5 AND p99_ratio>=1.75, "high",
    error_rate>(2*slo_allowed_error_pct) AND p99_ratio>=1.5, "medium",
    rate_drop_pct>15 OR error_rate>0.2 OR p99_ratio>=1.35, "low",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_open_apm_service_map_drill_operations_compare_canary_and_callers",
    severity=="high", "triage_downstream_edges_validate_histogram_rollup_and_detector_thresholds",
    severity=="medium", "notify_owner_review_sampling_instrumentation_and_slo_targets",
    true(), "observe_trend_review_metricsets_vs_raw_span2_metrics")
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,'service.name',service_name,""))))
     | eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")
     | eval slo_target=coalesce(slo_target,slo,"unspecified")
     | fields service_name owner_team slo_target env service_tier ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [ search index=catalog sourcetype=service_topology earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | dedup service_name
      | eval dependency_role=coalesce(dependency_role,topology_role,"unspecified")
      | fields service_name dependency_role ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,env,"unknown_environment")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| table _time, service_name, operation, environment, service_version, rate_rps, error_rate, p50_ms, p90_ms, p99_ms, dependency_in_count, top_caller_service, slo_burn_rate, severity, recommended_action, owner_team
```

savedsearches.conf sketch for packaging teams:

```ini
[obs_uc_13_5_7_apm_service_map_red_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch obs_uc_13_5_7_apm_service_map_red_health | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: when Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time climbing, materialize each lane into five-minute summaries keyed on service_name and operation, widen operational alerts to read summaries with earliest=-2h@h, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large fleets, split alerts by environment using where clauses driven by inventory service_tier columns.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Observability Cloud APM: pick a service you understand, open the service map for the same window as the Splunk search, and compare visual RED tiles to rate_rps, error_rate, and duration percentiles in the Splunk row. If Splunk lags the UI, verify indexer latency, HEC batching, and collector export intervals.

Validate metric normalization by sampling ten raw metric events in Search and confirming service_name resolution matches APM labels. If operation splits disagree, align span name conventions and http.route rules before trusting severity.

Validate dependency context by choosing a service with known upstreams, then confirm top_caller_service and dependency_in_count align with map badges for the same interval. If callers show none_observed while the map shows edges, fix parent_resource.service.name mapping at ingest.

Validate inventory and topology joins by running inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv alone and confirming keys cover tier-zero services. Run the catalog service_topology search with dedup to ensure new workloads appear.

Synthetic drill: inject coordinated rate drop, error share growth, and tail latency inflation in a lab tenant, confirm severity reaches critical within two buckets when all three conditions hold, then restore and confirm decay.

Negative test: on a calm window without deploys, critical severity should be rare for high-volume services unless baselines are miscalibrated; if noise persists, raise roll_rate_rps floors inside case logic.

Runbook linkage: tie results to dependency map drill habits, database triage guides, feature-flag consoles, and vendor status pages for third parties that frequently explain tail latency.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — RED regression after canary deploy: compare mixed service.version values, validate readiness and progressive delivery weights, roll forward or back based on map hotspots, and confirm critical severity aligns with simultaneous rate, error, and duration gates rather than a single signal flicker.

Case 2 — Library upgrade adds a new operation that splits rate across span names: reconcile operation cardinality in dashboards, adjust alert splits to meaningful http.route groups, and verify duration percentiles are not diluted by zero-duration synthetic spans.

Case 3 — Autoscaling lag during traffic surge: rate_rps may dip briefly while queues drain, error_rate can climb on timeouts, and p99_ms spikes on saturated instances; pair with autoscaler events and saturation metrics before declaring application logic defects.

Case 4 — Downstream database intermittent failure visible only on map edge: callee shows errors and latency while caller exhibits retry pressure; open the edge, validate pool health, and coordinate with data store owners instead of scaling callers blindly.

Case 5 — Missing instrumentation hides a hot operation: metric lanes show calm aggregates while span samples reveal concentrated latency in a rare span name; expand instrumentation, lower head sampling on that route if policy allows, and backfill MetricSets.

Case 6 — SLO target stale after feature-flag expansion: slo_burn_rate looks catastrophic because slo_target in inventory lags a new objective; refresh apm_service_inventory.csv and recalibrate slo_allowed_error_pct per tier.

Case 7 — Mis-tagged service.version splits metrics: duplicate service identities fragment rate_rps; normalize version attributes at the collector and dedupe inventory rows to restore coherent RED.

Case 8 — Sampling change pollutes histogram: percentile shifts appear without code changes; reconcile tail-sampling policies, compare histogram bucket series, and validate MonitorMetricSet windows against raw span2.metrics.

Case 9 — Retry storm masking real error: callers show elevated duration with modest error_rate while downstreams fail; inspect retry budgets, breaker settings, and map cycles before trusting a single service row.

Case 10 — Third-party vendor outage cascading: multiple services regress simultaneously with shared top_caller_service pointing at an integration hop; validate vendor health, regional endpoints, and circuit policies.

Case 11 — Garbage-collection pauses creating duration outliers: p99_ms spikes with bursty rate and stable error share; correlate JVM or runtime GC telemetry and compare to baseline_p99_ms before code rollback.

Case 12 — Sidecar mesh upgrade-related connection reset: dependency edges show churn with RST errors on client spans; align mesh version skew, mTLS rotation windows, and listener subset consistency across namespaces.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists operational columns including owner_team and recommended_action.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.7 Splunk APM service map RED fusion: span2.metrics rate and error lanes, duration percentiles, caller-callee dependency edges, optional version lane. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h bin=5m slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1 critical_combo_rate_drop_pct=40 critical_combo_err_pct=1.0 critical_combo_p99_mult=2.0")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name="spans.count" OR match(lower(metric_name),"^spans\.count$") OR match(lower(metric_name),"span2\.metrics.*spans\.count"))
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_operation,span_name,http_route,sf_operation,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,sf_environment,env,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,sf_service_version,release,"unknown_version")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval span_total=if(mv<0,-1*mv,mv)
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_span2_rate"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name="spans.count.error" OR (metric_name="spans.count" AND sf_error="true") OR match(lower(metric_name),"spans\.count\.error"))
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_operation,span_name,sf_operation,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,sf_environment,env,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,sf_service_version,release,"unknown_version")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval err_total=if(mv<0,-1*mv,mv)
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_span2_error"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name="spans.duration.ns.p50" OR metric_name="spans.duration.ns.p90" OR metric_name="spans.duration.ns.p99" OR metric_name="spans.duration.median" OR match(lower(metric_name),"spans\.duration"))
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_operation,span_name,sf_operation,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,sf_environment,env,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,sf_service_version,release,"unknown_version")
      | eval nsv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval p50_ms=if(match(lower(metric_name),"p50|median|p\\.50"),round(nsv/1000000,4),0)
      | eval p90_ms=if(match(lower(metric_name),"p90|p\\.90"),round(nsv/1000000,4),0)
      | eval p99_ms=if(match(lower(metric_name),"p99|p\\.99"),round(nsv/1000000,4),0)
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_span2_duration_pct"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span OR sourcetype=splunk:apm:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,service,""))))
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('parent_resource.service.name','caller.service.name',caller_service,upstream_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(name,span_name,operation_name,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unknown_version")
      | where len(callee)>0 AND len(caller)>0 AND caller!=callee
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats dc(caller) AS dependency_in_count values(caller) AS caller_mv BY _time callee operation environment service_version
      | eval top_caller_service=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(caller_mv),0),"none_observed")
      | rename callee AS service_name
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval lane="apm_dependency_edge"
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(name,span_name,"ALL")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,build_id,image_tag,"unknown_version")
      | eval span_total=0
      | eval err_total=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p90_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval dependency_in_count=0
      | eval top_caller_service="none_observed"
      | eval lane="apm_version_marker"
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_total err_total p50_ms p90_ms p99_ms dependency_in_count top_caller_service lane ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
| eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,"unknown_version")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(span_total) AS span_total sum(err_total) AS err_total
    max(p50_ms) AS p50_ms max(p90_ms) AS p90_ms max(p99_ms) AS p99_ms
    max(dependency_in_count) AS dependency_in_count
    values(top_caller_service) AS caller_mv
    BY _time service_name operation environment service_version
| eval top_caller_service=coalesce(mvindex(mvfilter(match(caller_mv,".+")),0),mvindex(caller_mv,0),"none_observed")
| eval rate_rps=round(span_total/300,6)
| eval span_total=if(isnull(span_total) OR span_total<1,1,span_total)
| eval error_rate=round(100*err_total/span_total,4)
| sort 0 + service_name + operation + environment + _time
| streamstats window=12 current=f global=f last(rate_rps) AS prior_rate_rps last(p99_ms) AS prior_p99_ms BY service_name operation environment service_version
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f avg(rate_rps) AS roll_rate_rps avg(error_rate) AS roll_err_rate BY service_name operation environment service_version
| eventstats avg(p99_ms) AS baseline_p99_ms BY service_name environment
| eval baseline_p99_ms=coalesce(baseline_p99_ms,prior_p99_ms,p99_ms,0.0001)
| eval rate_drop_pct=if(prior_rate_rps>0, round(100*(prior_rate_rps-rate_rps)/prior_rate_rps,2), 0)
| eval p99_ratio=if(baseline_p99_ms>0, round(p99_ms/baseline_p99_ms,3), 1)
| eval slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1
| eval slo_burn_rate=round(error_rate/nullif(slo_allowed_error_pct,0),4)
| eval severity=case(
    rate_drop_pct>40 AND error_rate>1.0 AND p99_ratio>=2.0 AND roll_rate_rps>0.01, "critical",
    rate_drop_pct>25 AND error_rate>0.5 AND p99_ratio>=1.75, "high",
    error_rate>(2*slo_allowed_error_pct) AND p99_ratio>=1.5, "medium",
    rate_drop_pct>15 OR error_rate>0.2 OR p99_ratio>=1.35, "low",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_open_apm_service_map_drill_operations_compare_canary_and_callers",
    severity=="high", "triage_downstream_edges_validate_histogram_rollup_and_detector_thresholds",
    severity=="medium", "notify_owner_review_sampling_instrumentation_and_slo_targets",
    true(), "observe_trend_review_metricsets_vs_raw_span2_metrics")
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| inputlookup apm_service_inventory.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,'service.name',service_name,""))))
     | eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")
     | eval slo_target=coalesce(slo_target,slo,"unspecified")
     | fields service_name owner_team slo_target env service_tier ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [ search index=catalog sourcetype=service_topology earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | dedup service_name
      | eval dependency_role=coalesce(dependency_role,topology_role,"unspecified")
      | fields service_name dependency_role ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,env,"unknown_environment")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| table _time, service_name, operation, environment, service_version, rate_rps, error_rate, p50_ms, p90_ms, p99_ms, dependency_in_count, top_caller_service, slo_burn_rate, severity, recommended_action, owner_team
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info latest(Application_State.severity) AS app_sev count FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Application_State.dest Application_State.app
| rename Application_State.dest AS service_key Application_State.app AS app_key
| join type=left max=0 service_key [| tstats summariesonly=true perc95(Web.status) AS p95_http_status FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Web.src | rename Web.src AS service_key]
| join type=left max=0 service_key [| tstats summariesonly=true perc95(Performance.total_processor_time) AS p95_cpu FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process | rename Performance.process AS service_key]
| table service_key app_key app_info app_sev p95_http_status p95_cpu
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Application_State summaries when health rows map service keys tied to service.name for routing RED fusion alarms; accelerate Web summaries for edge HTTP status facets used alongside trace HTTP attributes; accelerate Performance summaries when correlating host saturation with simultaneous rate, error, and duration regressions.
visualization: Heatmap of error_rate by service_name and operation, single-value tiles for critical severity counts, timeline of rate_rps paired with p99_ms, RED trio small-multiples by environment, drilldown table keyed on top_caller_service and dependency_in_count with links to APM service map URL templates, side panel for raw span2.metrics lanes in the same window.

references.title: Splunk Docs — View the service map in Splunk APM
Splunk Docs — Introduction to Splunk APM
Splunk Docs — Monitor applications with Splunk APM
Splunk Docs — Create detectors to trigger alerts

knownFalsePositives: Chaos engineering tests that inject coordinated failure, latency, and traffic throttling can trip simultaneous RED gates without customer impact; segregate labeled namespaces and change calendars before paging service owners. Scheduled batch windows that legitimately collapse request rate can look like a rate crisis while error share is meaningless on tiny denominators; require roll_rate_rps floors and inventory tier filters for interactive paths. Machine-learning or A/B model rollouts sometimes shift traffic between operations and split metrics across span names, creating apparent rate drops on one operation while another absorbs load; validate experiment metadata before rollback. Sample-rate or tail-sampling edits change histogram populations and can inflate p99_ms or deflate rate without code changes; reconcile collector policies and compare raw span2.metrics with MonitorMetricSets. Weekend or holiday traffic shapes can diverge from weekday baselines and move p99_ratio without defects; compare like-for-like seasons or use environment-specific baselines. Canary analysis windows with partial fleet traffic routinely depress rate_rps while errors concentrate on canary instances; pair with progressive delivery dashboards. Third-party maintenance that returns synthetic health errors on integration edges can raise error_rate without core service bugs; map vendor routes and suppressible dependencies. Autoscaling cold-start minutes can depress measured throughput while latency tails spike; correlate kube events before simultaneous RED pages. Mis-joined inventory rows that duplicate service keys can distort slo_burn_rate context; audit apm_service_inventory.csv keys quarterly.
hardware: Kubernetes-hosted microservices, Linux or containerized OpenTelemetry Collector pools, optional service mesh sidecars, Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier at 9.2 or newer indexing metrics and spans mirrored from Observability Cloud.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.1 duration-percentile-first anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 error-rate-first burn narratives, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness and orphan-span analytics, pure real-user monitoring without distributed telemetry, or infrastructure-only CPU charts lacking service map context. Does not automatically change sampling policies, detector programs, or deployment traffic weights.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, ingest spans.count and spans.count.error metrics plus duration percentile metrics for a checkout service while injecting coordinated rate drop, rising error share, and doubled p99_ms against baseline for two consecutive five-minute buckets; confirm obs_uc_13_5_7_apm_service_map_red_health returns critical severity with non-null top_caller_service after dependency spans land and inventory joins resolve owner_team.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when rate_rps returns near prior windows, error_rate falls below slo_allowed_error_pct guidance, and p99_ms returns under baseline ratio thresholds with stable roll_rate_rps, confirm severities return to low or medium for tier-one services and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

evidence: Saved search obs_uc_13_5_7_apm_service_map_red_health with scheduled execution, dashboard drilldowns tied to Splunk Observability Cloud APM service map views, and weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted observability_evidence index.
